title: Wilyer Abreu joins elite Red Sox company following his back-to-back multi-HR games
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit two multi-home run games consecutively for the Red Sox against the Tampa Bay Rays.
This makes Abreu the first Red Sox player to achieve this since Mookie Betts in June 2016.
Abreu's performance helped push the Red Sox over .500, with a chance for their 13th consecutive win.

title: Red Sox extend season-altering win streak to 12 games in epic comeback fashion
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 12 games with a comeback victory over the Tampa Bay Rays.
Wilyer Abreu's two home runs, including a game-winning shot, led the Red Sox to a 9-6 win at Fenway Park.
Aroldis Chapman saved the game despite a shaky ninth inning, helping Boston maintain the AL's final wild-card spot.

title: Report: LeBron James ‘hates’ Celtics, would rank them last on his list of teams to join
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James reportedly ranks the Boston Celtics last on his list of potential teams to join.
ESPN's Brian Windhorst claims James dislikes Boston due to long-standing rivalries and accusations of racism.
The Miami Heat, Cleveland Cavaliers, and Golden State Warriors are rumored as James's top free agency options.

title: Tom Brady busy at Fanatics Fest, from a face slap to showing off his arm
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tom Brady went viral for slapping Logan Paul during a panel at Fanatics Fest NYC 2026.
Brady jokingly insulted Paul on social media after the incident, referencing a prior flag football game.
Aside from the slap, Brady participated in activities like throwing a baseball and golfing.

title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men are charged with kidnapping and assaulting a 28-year-old man near Winter Park playground.
The suspects, aged 21 to 34, allegedly restrained and beat the victim with a wooden stake, causing multiple injuries.
The men face charges including kidnapping, assault, and assault with a dangerous weapon; their arraignment is scheduled for Monday.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: ICE arrested Maria Rosales and David Ardila at Logan Airport on July 10 amid increased ICE activity.
Rosales faces deportation due to a 2017 removal order; Ardila was detained for overstaying his visa.
Attorney Todd Pomerleau seeks release for both, citing pending DACA and asylum applications.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Watch 'Interstellar' (2014) for similar themes to Nolan’s 'The Odyssey', featuring space-time adventures on Hulu and Paramount+.
'Lawrence of Arabia' (1962) mirrors the epic journey of Odysseus with Peter O'Toole in the lead, available on Netflix.
'O Brother, Where Art Thou?' (2000) offers a humorous take on 'The Odyssey' featuring Coen Brothers’ storytelling on Criterion Channel.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox extend their winning streak to 11 after sweeping a doubleheader against the Rays.
Boston's win streak is the team's longest since 2016, improving their record to 48-48.
The Red Sox now hold the third American League wild-card spot, slightly ahead of the Mariners and Twins.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez could start for the Red Sox on July 21 after recovering from a groin strain.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo are making progress in their rehabilitation and could return this season.
Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet, and others remain shut down with no timeline for returning to the Red Sox.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit three times, including a home run and RBI, leading Boston to victory in the first game.
Caleb Durbin went 5 for 7, scoring two runs, pressuring Tampa Bay's pitchers despite not hitting home runs.
Jake Bennett pitched 6 scoreless innings with one hit allowed, helping preserve the bullpen for Boston's doubleheader sweep.
